Redirect stdout and stderr independently, read both asynchronously, wait for completion and treat Process.ExitCode as the only native success/failure truth.
Pass the two authorized secret values only through the child process environment plus WSLENV; never place them in command-line arguments, logs or evidence.

applies_when: The initial durable journal implementation opened the caller-supplied journal path with os.OpenFile without first rejecting a direct symbolic-link target.

next_action: Complete exact-head CI for PR #603 and merge only when Node Validation, race tests, Project Item Gate and governance checks all pass. Then implement persistent cryptographic identity and enrollment under Issue #289 while reusing the existing ADCP runner registry.
solution_pattern: Inspect an existing journal target with os.Lstat and reject a direct symbolic link before opening it.
Reject existing targets that are not regular files.
Re-check the opened descriptor with file.Stat and fail closed if it is not a regular file.
Apply file.Chmod(0600) to both newly created and pre-existing journal files before replay or append.

do_not_repeat: Do not rely only on runtime Config::set to define a canonical runner operation.
Do not remove AGENT_STATUS_EVIDENCE from the runner because it is required by the existing Golden Agent Stage 0 contract.
Do not create another activation request before exact production deployment proof.
Do not bypass RUNNER_CAPABILITY_SET_INVALID by weakening ExternalRunnerGatewayService validation.
Do not proceed to end-to-end rollover while runtime 1.2.0 remains unproven.
next_action: Validate and merge PR #621 with exact-head guard, obtain exact production deployment proof for its merge SHA, then execute no more than one new finalization activation request and independently verify runtime 1.2.0 before disposable end-to-end acceptance.
solution_pattern: Add AGENT_STATUS_EVIDENCE directly to application/config/external_runner.php allowed_operations so cached and deployed configuration contains the complete canonical set.

applies_when: A controlled chat reported that the startup read_order was completed even though the evidence only established that files had been contacted or partially returned.
The previous attestation treated opening a file and receiving an initial connector response as equivalent to reading the complete substantive content.
do_not_repeat: Do not report read_order completion merely because every path was opened or fetched.
Do not return FULL_READ_ORDER_CONTENT_READ=PASS when any response is truncated, partial, paginated or missing later line ranges.
Do not substitute a summary or metadata response for the complete file content.
Do not omit later JSONL records.
evidence_refs: Owner directive in controlled chat on 2026-07-12
next_action: Validate and merge PR #664, then confirm the public startup mirror contains the full-content read rule and FULL_READ_ORDER_CONTENT_READ attestation field.
solution_pattern: Define complete reading as retrieval and processing of the full substantive content from beginning to end.
Declare that filenames, metadata, SHA values, summaries, first chunks and truncated output do not count as completion.
Require continuation through all remaining pages, line ranges and JSONL records.
Add FULL_READ_ORDER_CONTENT_READ as a mandatory startup attestation field and forbid PASS until every read_order file is complete.

applies_when: The first merged activation request for the existing windows-desktop-runner-01 copied the validated 1.2.0 source and started the existing ELEMENTARY Desktop Runner task, but no fresh health observation was published and the task returned result 1.
The activation timed out with WINDOWS_DESKTOP_RUNNER_HEALTH_FRESHNESS_TIMEOUT and correctly restored runtime 1.1.0.
do_not_repeat: Do not invoke python.exe directly under ErrorActionPreference=Stop for the governed Windows Desktop Runner.
Do not treat native stderr as process failure truth.
Do not use LASTEXITCODE when a governed Process object can provide the exact native exit code.
Do not create a second task, runner identity, activation request or browser-runner path.
Do not continue to disposable end-to-end rollover until Apply and independent runtime inventory both pass.
next_action: Complete exact-head validation of PR #619, merge the repair, create exactly one new finalization activation request, observe non-cancellable Apply, independently prove runtime 1.2.0 and all three governed chat operations, then execute the disposable end-to-end autonomous rollover acceptance chain.
solution_pattern: Launch the fixed Python 3.12 executable through System.Diagnostics.ProcessStartInfo with UseShellExecute=false and CreateNoWindow=true.
Redirect stdout and stderr independently, read both streams asynchronously, wait for process completion and use Process.ExitCode as the only native success/failure truth.
Append bounded stdout and stderr to the existing fixed task logs only after process completion and always write the task exit marker from Process.ExitCode.
Add a source contract that requires ProcessStartInfo and rejects direct ampersand Python invocation, native 1>>/2>> redirection and LASTEXITCODE.

Separate PRODUCT_AVAILABLE, ACCOUNT_VISIBLE, PROJECT_VERIFIED and RUNTIME_ACTIVE for every Plus capability claim.
Use Plus plugins/apps and ChatGPT Work only when the exact account surface is visible and authorized.

applies_when: An exact-SHA cPanel deployment could be queued or completed correctly while the controlling bridge still failed closed for three distinct observability and idempotency reasons: a transient HTTP 503 during read-only request-status polling, an exact pre-mutation DEPLOYMENT_ALREADY_RUNNING lock collision with the same canonical deployment, and success writeback logic that emitted stale failure fallback markers even when bound deployment and System Twin evidence were PASS.
do_not_repeat: Do not repeat prepare or execute after a queued request merely because status readback returned HTTP 503.
Do not create a second deployment path or scheduler to bypass canonical cPanel lock contention.
Do not accept DEPLOYMENT_ALREADY_RUNNING without proving the same exact target SHA through read-only production status and public proof.
Do not infer final acceptance from Issue comment count or a single writeback.
Do not publish stale failure fallback markers when fully bound evidence status is PASS.
Do not expose raw server results, credentials, JWTs, keys or environment dumps while diagnosing terminal evidence.
next_action: For future exact-SHA cPanel bridge failures, first inspect the immutable artifact and classify the failure as status-read transport, exact pre-mutation lock collision, or real terminal deployment error. Apply bounded read-only recovery only to the first two classes; preserve one-shot mutation and fail closed for every other result. Require bridge, production SHA, System Twin parity, neutral success markers and independent diagnostic to PASS on the same current-main SHA before closing the work item.
solution_pattern: Keep prepare and execute one-shot and exact-SHA bound. Retry only transient HTTP 503 responses from read-only status polling within the existing bound. When a terminal request contains only the exact privacy-safe DEPLOYMENT_ALREADY_RUNNING marker, do not execute again; observe the existing canonical deployment through bounded read-only production status and require the public proof for the same exact SHA. In the single Issue writeback, emit failure=none only when fully bound deployment status is PASS and system_twin_failure=none only when fully bound parity status is PASS; retain all existing fail-closed attribution otherwise.

applies_when: Enrollment Apply run 29248354185 reached the authorized source/request gates and timed out with one active run before identity creation.
The user-context script stopped the existing runner before waiting for server-side active runs to drain, which could prevent the active run from finishing inside the bounded wait.
do_not_repeat: Do not stop the runner before active work has drained.
Do not create identity until a post-stop zero-active-runs readback passes.
Do not perform rollback mutation when the pre-mutation state is already unchanged.
Do not retry Apply without fresh Linux and Windows proofs on one exact post-fix main SHA.
next_action: Merge after exact-head validation, then run Linux proof followed by Windows proof on the same exact main SHA before one governed Apply.
solution_pattern: Keep the existing runner live while waiting a bounded interval for active runs to reach zero.
Stop the runner only after zero is observed, then immediately re-read status and fail on any post-stop race before identity creation.
When a failure occurs before identity/server mutation and the runner was not stopped, record HMAC-only unchanged instead of interrupting the runner through rollback.
Preserve governed unbind-before-local-delete rollback for every failure after identity/server mutation.

applies_when: Enrollment Apply run 29251555563 proved the legacy runner remained healthy and reachable but active_runs stayed at one for the full bounded pre-identity drain.

The prior Windows proof validated installed signer/source capability but did not perform the interactive-user read-only production readiness check, so a two-PASS pair could still admit an Apply while active execution blocked enrollment.
do_not_repeat: Do not count Windows proof as PASS when active_runs is nonzero or missing.
Do not stop or cancel an active run to force enrollment readiness.
Do not submit Apply without a fresh Linux then Windows PASS pair on one exact main SHA.
Do not expose raw authentication material or key data.
next_action: Merge after exact-head validation; run Linux then Windows proof on one exact main SHA. If Windows readiness fails, remain HMAC-only and wait. Apply exactly once only after both PASS.
solution_pattern: Require the interactive-user read-only preflight to prove active_runs equals zero.
Execute that preflight as part of the Windows job after source/installed-signer validation for both proof and Apply commands.
Keep the gate non-mutating: no runner stop, identity creation, registry mutation, work execution or production acceptance.
Preserve the in-Apply race guard and governed rollback ordering.

applies_when: A full-file GitHub contents update was prepared from a partial read of ExternalRunnerGatewayService.php.
The resulting commit replaced the canonical gateway implementation and removed claim, checkpoint, evidence and controlled-capability lifecycle logic.
do_not_repeat: Do not replace a file unless its complete content has been read or an exact blob/tree operation is used.
Do not infer omitted portions of a long file.
Do not continue after an unexpectedly large diff.
Do not claim recovery until the exact previous blob SHA is read back from main.
next_action: Review canonical entry before acting.
solution_pattern: Inspect every large-file replacement commit immediately and reject unexpected line or method deletion.
Never use a partial line-range read as replacement content.
For a one-commit tip error with no later commits, restore the exact parent ref and verify the original blob SHA.
Prefer a new small service or adapter over editing a large central file when the available mutation interface only supports full replacement.
